consistency.name_and_slug: Consistent. The name 'Countercharm' matches the slug 'bard-countercharm'.
consistency.level_and_class: Consistent. Level 6 feature belonging to the 'bard' class.

quality.description_clarity: High. The description clearly outlines the trigger (action to start performance), duration (until end of next turn), effect (advantage on saves vs. frightened/charmed), range (30 feet), and limitations (must hear, ends if incapacitated/silenced or voluntarily ended).
quality.mechanical_impact: Moderate to High. Providing advantage on saving throws against two very common and impactful conditions (charmed and frightened) for multiple allies is a significant defensive boon, especially at mid-levels.

mechanics.range_and_area: 30 feet radius centered on the user.
mechanics.concentration_or_duration: Duration is fixed to the end of the next turn, similar to a short-duration concentration effect but without the concentration requirement, making it durable unless specific end conditions are met.

suggestions: No mandatory changes. The feature is mechanically sound and thematic.
It is worth noting that since this does not require concentration, a Bard can use this while concentrating on another spell, which is a strong design point.
